tasks.subject: [fix-relay-sse-delivery-pipeline] [P3] Update architecture documentation
tasks.description:
## Objective

Update documentation to reflect the subscribe-first handshake pattern and pending buffer mechanism.

## Files

### 1. `contributing/architecture.md`

In the Relay section, add a subsection documenting the subscribe-first handshake:

```markdown
### Subscribe-First SSE Handshake

When Relay is enabled, the SSE delivery pipeline uses a subscribe-first handshake to prevent message loss:

1. Client opens EventSource to `GET /api/sessions/:id/stream?clientId={clientId}`
2. Server registers SSE client and subscribes to `relay.human.console.{clientId}`
3. Server sends `stream_ready` SSE event to confirm subscription is active
4. Client receives `stream_ready`, then sends `POST /api/sessions/:id/messages`
5. Server publishes to `relay.agent.{sessionId}`, CCA processes, publishes response chunks to `relay.human.console.{clientId}`
6. SessionBroadcaster receives chunks via subscription and writes to SSE stream

Defense-in-depth: A pending buffer in SubscriptionRegistry captures messages published during the brief window before a subscriber registers (5-second TTL). Messages are drained immediately when the subscriber connects.
```

### 2. `specs/fix-relay-sse-backpressure/04-implementation.md`

Add a note at the end referencing this spec as the follow-up fix:

```markdown
## Follow-Up

The backpressure fix addressed write queue serialization but did not resolve upstream delivery issues. See `specs/fix-relay-sse-delivery-pipeline/` for the complete fix addressing EventSource lifecycle, subscribe-first handshake, pending buffer, and terminal done event.
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `contributing/architecture.md` documents the subscribe-first handshake
- [ ] `specs/fix-relay-sse-backpressure/04-implementation.md` references this spec
- [ ] No broken markdown links
